name: ECDLP verified knowledge graph
purpose: A machine-readable, navigable index of the machine-checked (Lean 4 + Mathlib, no sorry, no axioms) structure of the ECDLP landscape for secp256k1: proved theorems, their import dependencies, the corpus claims they verify, and the formalization barriers that bound them. Built for a future automated reasoner to load the known-and-verified frontier without re-deriving it.
invariant: Every theorem listed is accepted by the Lean kernel with no sorry and no added axioms. Truth is decided only by the kernel; this graph is derived from the ledger, it does not assert proofs.

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_double_x_eq_ќ¶вВВ_div_ќ®вВВSq`, `secp256k1_ќ¶вВВ
theorems.claim: Multiplication-by-2 `x`-coordinate formula in Mathlib's canonical division polynomials (`secp256k1_double_x_eq_ќ¶вВВ_div_ќ®вВВSq`: for a non-2-torsion point `P=(x,y)` on `y=x¬≥+7`, the doubled `x`-coordinate `addX x x (slope x x y y) = (ќ¶ 2)(x)/(ќ®вВВSq)(x)` вАФ with Mathlib's own `ќ¶`/`ќ®Sq` division polynomials, `ќ¶ 2 = XвБівИТ56X` (`secp256k1_ќ¶вВВ`), `ќ®вВВSq = 4X¬≥+28 = 4y`. This is the base case `n=2` of the general multiplication formula `x([n]P)=ќ¶вВЩ/ќ®SqвВЩ` вАФ the engine of the general `ѕИвВЩ`-vanishing вЯЇ `n`-torsion bridge (`notes/FOUNDATIONS.md` rung 4). Unlike the per-`n` bridges (which used ad-hoc reduced polynomials like `3xвБі+84x`), this states the *canonical* `ќ¶вВЩ/ќ®SqвВЩ` shape. The general-`n` formula, by induction on the division-polynomial recurrence, is the missing Mathlib rung вАФ absent from v4.31, present only in a stalled upstream PR)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_torsion_iff_principal
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing foundations, rung 1 вАФ torsion вЯЇ principal divisor (`secp256k1_torsion_iff_principal`: a point `P` of secp256k1 is `n`-torsion `вЯЇ n вАҐ toClass P = 0`, i.e. iff the divisor `n¬Ј([P] вИТ [O])` is principal in the coordinate ring `F[secp256k1]`. Obtained by transporting `n`-torsion across Mathlib's AbelвАУJacobi map `toClass : W.Point вЖТ+ Additive (ClassGroup W.CoordinateRing)` вАФ the injective embedding `E вЖ™ Pic` on which Mathlib *builds* the group law вАФ via `toClass_eq_zero` + `map_nsmul`. This is the first genuine rung of the Weil pairing construction: principality of `n¬Ј([P] вИТ [O])` is the existence precondition for the Miller function `f_P`. Key finding: the divisor-class substrate (`toClass`, `FunctionField`, `rootsOfUnity`) is already in Mathlib, so the pairing is more reachable than "from zero" вАФ remaining rungs (extract `f_P`, evaluate at divisors, Weil reciprocity, bilinear `eвВЩвЖТќЉвВЩ`) are the open frontier, `notes/FOUNDATIONS.md`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_miller_function_exists
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing foundations, rung 2 вАФ the Miller function exists (`secp256k1_miller_function_exists`: for a nonzero `n`-torsion point `P=(x,y)` of secp256k1, there exists `f_P` in the function field `F(secp256k1)` generating the principal fractional ideal `(вЯ®XвИТx, YвИТyвЯ©)вБњ = (XYIdeal' h)вБњ` вАФ the Miller function, whose divisor is `n¬Ј([P] вИТ [O])`. Extracted from rung 1: `n`-torsion trivializes the class `ClassGroup.mk ((XYIdeal' h)вБњ) = (mk (XYIdeal' h))вБњ = 1`, and Mathlib's `ClassGroup.mk_eq_one_iff` turns a trivial class into a principal ideal, whose generator is `f_P`. Rung W2 of the Weil sub-ladder вАФ the input to Weil reciprocity / the pairing `eвВЩ`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_miller_function_unique
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing foundations, rung 3 вАФ the Miller function is unique up to a unit (`secp256k1_miller_function_unique`: two generators `f, g` of the same principal ideal `(XYIdeal' h)вБњ` differ by a unit of the coordinate ring, `вИГ u : F[secp256k1]Ћ£, u вАҐ f = g` вАФ the well-definedness input for the Weil pairing `eвВЩ` (its value must not depend on the chosen Miller function). Representative-independence half of rung W3. Proof designed by the Fable model and kernel-verified вАФ a piloted "strong-model + Lean-kernel" loop: Fable read the Mathlib source, identified `Submodule.span_singleton_eq_span_singleton`, and produced the proof, which the kernel accepted)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalAt`, `evalAt_surjective`, `evalAt_ker
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing infrastructure вАФ point-evaluation homomorphism (`evalAt`: for a rational point `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field, evaluation of a regular function at `P` as a ring hom `F[E] вЖТ+* F`, built from Mathlib's `quotientXYIdealEquiv` (`F[E]/вЯ®XвИТx,YвИТyвЯ© вЙГ F`); `evalAt_surjective` (every value attained вАФ `P` is `F`-rational) and `evalAt_ker` (kernel `= вЯ®XвИТx, YвИТyвЯ©`, the maximal ideal of `P` вАФ the functions vanishing at `P`). The first rung of the function-evaluation layer Mathlib lacks (v4.31 has no rational-function evaluation API and `F[E]` is not known to be Dedekind there); regular-function evaluation now exists, the rational extension via localization is next. Unblocks the Weil pairing's `f_P(D_Q)` step)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_isMaximal
theorems.claim: A rational point is a closed point (maximal ideal) (`xyIdeal_isMaximal`: for `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field, the ideal `вЯ®XвИТx, YвИТyвЯ©` of `F[E]` is maximal вАФ since the quotient `F[E]/вЯ®XвИТx,YвИТyвЯ© вЙГ F` is a field. Establishes each `F`-rational point as a closed point of the affine curve; the prerequisite for localizing at `P` (the local ring `F[E]_P`) to evaluate rational functions regular at `P` вАФ the next infrastructure rung toward the Weil pairing's `f_P(D_Q)`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_algebraMap
theorems.claim: evalRatAt` extends `evalAt` (`evalRatAt_algebraMap`: for `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field and a regular function `r вИИ F[E]`, the value of its image in the local ring `F[E]_P = Localization.AtPrime вЯ®XвИТx,YвИТyвЯ©` under `evalRatAt` equals its direct value `evalAt h r`. The correctness certificate that rational-function evaluation genuinely extends regular-function evaluation вАФ the residue of the localization at `P` coincides *definitionally* with the quotient by `P`'s maximal ideal. Closes the evaluation layer before the Miller function `f_P` is evaluated; proof developed on the warm-server `lake env lean` loop)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_ne_of_x_ne
theorems.claim: Distinct x-coordinates вЗТ distinct closed points (`xyIdeal_ne_of_x_ne`: rational points on a Weierstrass curve with `xвВБ вЙ† xвВВ` give different maximal ideals `вЯ®XвИТx, YвИТyвЯ©` of `F[E]`. Proof: `XClass xвВБ вИТ XClass xвВВ = algebraMap F F[E] (xвВВвИТxвВБ)` is a unit when `xвВБ вЙ† xвВВ`; equal ideals would force that unit into a maximal (proper) ideal вАФ contradiction. The divisor-support separation the Weil pairing needs вАФ the points of `D_Q` chosen to compute `f_P(D_Q)` must avoid `P`, `O`, which requires distinct points to be genuinely distinct closed points; scouted + proved on the warm-server loop)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_ne_of_ne` (+ `xyIdeal_ne_of_y_ne`)
theorems.claim: Distinct points вЗТ distinct closed points (full separation) (`xyIdeal_ne_of_ne`: for rational points `(xвВБ,yвВБ) вЙ† (xвВВ,yвВВ)` on a Weierstrass curve the maximal ideals `вЯ®XвИТx, YвИТyвЯ©` differ вАФ combining the x-coordinate case with the `YClass` companion `xyIdeal_ne_of_y_ne` (`yвВБ вЙ† yвВВ вЗТ` distinct ideals, same unit-in-a-maximal-ideal argument on `Y вИТ ¬Ј`). The complete divisor-support-separation lemma for the Weil pairing: any two distinct rational points are distinct closed points of `F[E]`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_eq_zero_iff
theorems.claim: Weil W3 вАФ zero locus of point-evaluation (`evalRatAt_eq_zero_iff`: a rational function regular at `P` vanishes at `P` iff it lies in the maximal ideal of the local ring `F[E]_P`. From `evalRatAt = residueFieldEquiv вИШ residue`: residue-field iso injective, so `evalRatAt r = 0 вЖФ residue r = 0 вЖФ r вИИ рЭФ™`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_ne_zero_iff_isUnit
theorems.claim: Weil W3 вАФ nonvanishing criterion at a point (`evalRatAt_ne_zero_iff_isUnit`: a rational function regular at `P` is nonzero at `P` iff it is a unit of the local ring `F[E]_P` вАФ exactly the condition to evaluate a Miller function `f_P` to a nonzero value in `рЭФљ_p`, off its zero/pole support. Completes the *point-evaluation* half of Weil rung W3; `f_P(D_Q)` over a whole divisor and Weil reciprocity (W4) remain genuine Mathlib gaps вАФ see `BARRIERS.md`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi3_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ѕИвВГ root вЯЇ Miller divisor `3¬Ј([P]вИТ[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi3_root_iff_class_torsion`: for a nonzero affine `P=(x,y)` of secp256k1, `(ѕИ 3).evalEval x y = 0 вЖФ 3 вАҐ toClass P = 0` вАФ the 3-division polynomial vanishes at `P` iff the AbelвАУJacobi class is 3-torsion, i.e. iff `3¬Ј([P]вИТ[O])` is principal. Welds the division-polynomial torsion tower to Weil divisor-class rung 1 at their shared middle `3вАҐP=0`; the first result letting a computable coordinate condition `ѕИ 3 = 0` *decide* the divisor-theoretic principality the Miller function needs)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi5_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ѕИвВЕ root вЯЇ Miller divisor `5¬Ј([P]вИТ[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi5_root_iff_class_torsion`: the `n=5` analogue вАФ `(ѕИ 5).evalEval x y = 0 вЖФ 5 вАҐ toClass P = 0`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi7_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ѕИвВЗ root вЯЇ Miller divisor `7¬Ј([P]вИТ[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi7_root_iff_class_torsion`: the `n=7` analogue вАФ `(ѕИ 7).evalEval x y = 0 вЖФ 7 вАҐ toClass P = 0`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_grp_nsmul_eq_zero` (+ the `Module (ZMod n) вЖ•вЯ®GвЯ©` instance, `secp256k1GвВЩ`)
theorems.claim: The concrete secp256k1 subgroup `вЯ®GвЯ©` is a `ZMod n`-module (`secp256k1_grp_nsmul_eq_zero`: every point of `вЯ®GвЯ© = zmultiples secp256k1G` is annihilated by `n`, so `вЯ®GвЯ©` carries a `Module (ZMod n)` structure built via `AddCommGroup.zmodModule`. This realises the abstract discrete-log model on a genuine elliptic-curve group вАФ Mathlib provides no automatic `ZMod n`-module on a cyclic subgroup; it must be constructed from the exponent bound, which the weak keystone `addOrderOf secp256k1G = n` supplies. The bridge that lets every abstract protocol theorem instantiate on real `WeierstrassCurve.Affine.Point` values)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_dh_agree`, `secp256k1_schnorr_verify`, `secp256k1_taproot_tweak_verify`, `secp256k1_adaptor_complete
theorems.claim: Deployed signature/agreement protocols are correct on the concrete secp256k1 group (the abstract `Ecdlp.Schnorr` correctness theorems instantiated at the concrete carrier `вЖ•вЯ®GвЯ©` with base point `secp256k1GвВЩ`, about genuine curve points: DiffieвАУHellman agreement `secp256k1_dh_agree`, Schnorr/EdDSA verification `secp256k1_schnorr_verify`, Bitcoin Taproot key-tweak `secp256k1_taproot_tweak_verify` (BIP-341), adaptor-signature completeness `secp256k1_adaptor_complete` (atomic swaps / Lightning PTLCs). Contingent only on `ord(G) = n` + the abstract algebra вАФ the honest content: the real secp256k1 protocols verify on the actual curve subgroup, not only an abstract module)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_threshold_schnorr_aggregate`, `secp256k1_musig_key_aggregate`, `secp256k1_feldman_vss_verify`, `secp256k1_threshold_elgamal_combine`, `secp256k1_schnorr_batch_verify
theorems.claim: *(instantiation, multiparty protocols)* the same concrete-`вЯ®GвЯ©` realization for the multi-signer / threshold protocols вАФ aggregate Schnorr / Taproot multisig `secp256k1_threshold_schnorr_aggregate` (MuSig/FROST), MuSig2 key aggregation `secp256k1_musig_key_aggregate`, Feldman VSS share verification `secp256k1_feldman_vss_verify` (DKG), threshold ElGamal partial-decryption combination `secp256k1_threshold_elgamal_combine`, batch Schnorr verification `secp256k1_schnorr_batch_verify

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_grp_card`, `secp256k1_grp_isAddCyclic
theorems.claim: The secp256k1 crypto subgroup `вЯ®GвЯ©` is cyclic of order exactly `n` (`secp256k1_grp_card`: `Nat.card вЯ®GвЯ© = n`, from `Nat.card (zmultiples g) = addOrderOf g` + the weak keystone; `secp256k1_grp_isAddCyclic`: `IsAddCyclic вЯ®GвЯ©`. The group-object-level statement of "вЯ®GвЯ© is a concrete cyclic group of order `n`" вАФ no Hasse/`#E`, only `addOrderOf G = n`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_dlogEquiv` (+ `secp256k1_dlogHom`, `_injective`, `_surjective`, `secp256k1_gen_addOrderOf`)
theorems.claim: The discrete-log map is a group isomorphism `ZMod n вЙГ+ вЯ®GвЯ©` (`secp256k1_dlogEquiv`: base-point multiplication `c вЖ¶ c¬ЈG` is an additive-group isomorphism between the scalar ring `ZMod n` and the concrete secp256k1 subgroup `вЯ®GвЯ©` вАФ the exact group-theoretic content of the ECDLP setting: the discrete log `dlog = (c вЖ¶ c¬ЈG)вБї` is a well-defined bijection. Its *computational* inversion is the open ECDLP hardness conjecture; this asserts only the unconditional bijection, via injective (`c.val < n = addOrderOf G`) + surjective (`zmultiples`) on the `ZMod n`-module `вЯ®GвЯ©`. Built with `AddEquiv.ofBijective`)

theorems.name: WeierstrassCurve.Affine.instFinitePoint
theorems.claim: Affine Weierstrass points are finite over any finite ring вАФ general lemma (Mathlib-gap upstream candidate) (`instFinitePoint`: `Finite W.Point` for every `W : WeierstrassCurve.Affine R` with `[CommRing R] [Finite R]`, via `Point вЖ™ Option (R √Ч R)`. Weakest hypotheses вАФ no `Field`/`Nontrivial`. Mathlib v4.31.0 has no `Finite`/`Fintype` instance on any `Point` variant (gap confirmed against pinned source); natural upstream home `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/EllipticCurve/Affine/Point.lean`. Subsumes the two per-curve instances below, now one-line `inferInstance` corollaries. Pure-kernel)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.instFiniteSecp256k1Point
theorems.claim: secp256k1 rational-point group is finite (`instFiniteSecp256k1Point`: `Finite secp256k1.toAffine.Point`, now a one-line `inferInstance` corollary of the general `WeierstrassCurve.Affine.instFinitePoint` since `ZMod Secp256k1.p` is a finite commutative ring. Makes Mathlib's `Nat.card E.toAffine.Point` (as used in `EllipticCurve/LFunction.lean`) a real quantity rather than junk `0`. Pure-kernel, no native_decide)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_n_dvd_card_point
theorems.claim: n вИ£ #E(рЭФљ_p)` вАФ base-point order divides the curve cardinality (`secp256k1_n_dvd_card_point`: `Secp256k1.n вИ£ Nat.card secp256k1.toAffine.Point`, by Lagrange on `вЯ®GвЯ©` (order the proved `n`) + finiteness. The first verified fact about `#E` itself вАФ all prior point-counting stayed inside `вЯ®GвЯ©`. Pins `#E вИИ {n, 2n, 3n, вА¶}`; the strong keystone `#E = n` is now proved on top, curve-specifically without Hasse/Schoof (`CurveCardinalityExact.lean`). Pure-kernel)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_card_point_eq_n`, `secp256k1_no_nonzero_two_torsion`, `secp256k1_card_point_le
theorems.claim: The exact curve cardinality `#E(рЭФљ_p) = n` вАФ cofactor 1, without Hasse or Schoof (`secp256k1_card_point_eq_n`: `Nat.card secp256k1.toAffine.Point = Secp256k1.n`). A curve-specific certificate that upgrades `n вИ£ #E` to equality using the special structure of `E : Y = X¬≥ + 7` over `p вЙ° 1 (mod 3)`: `#E вЙ§ 2p+1` (`secp256k1_card_point_le`, вЙ§2 `y` per `x`) and `2p+1 < 3n` force `#E вИИ {n, 2n}`; `2n` (even) is excluded because additive Cauchy would yield a nonzero 2-torsion point, but `E[2] = {O}` (`secp256k1_no_nonzero_two_torsion`: `2вАҐP=0 вЯє P=0`, since `y=0 вЯє x¬≥=вИТ7` and `вИТ7` is not a cube in `рЭФљ_p`, `(вИТ7)^((pвИТ1)/3)вЙ†1`). Removes the last standing assumption behind Units 1вАУ2 (the published order `n` as `#E`). Uses `native_decide` for the 256-bit non-cube leaf and the literal arithmetic (`Lean.ofReduceBool`); numeric facts independently re-checked in `scripts/certs/cardinality_secp256k1.py`, prints `CERT_OK`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_grp_eq_top`, `secp256k1_point_isAddCyclic`, `secp256k1_mem_zmultiples`, `secp256k1_glvHom_eq_zsmul_unconditional
theorems.claim: The whole point group equals `вЯ®GвЯ©` вАФ `E(рЭФљ_p)` is cyclic, `glvPoint = [ќї]` unconditional (`secp256k1_grp_eq_top`: `secp256k1Grp = вК§`, from `#вЯ®GвЯ© = n = #E` via `AddSubgroup.eq_top_of_card_eq`). Upgrades every `вЯ®GвЯ©`-only result to the full rational-point group now that `#E = n` is proved: `secp256k1_point_isAddCyclic` (`IsAddCyclic E(рЭФљ_p)`, an instance), `secp256k1_mem_zmultiples` (every point is a multiple of `G`), and `secp256k1_glvHom_eq_zsmul_unconditional` (the GLV eigenvalue property with the `[IsAddCyclic]` hypothesis now discharged вАФ `glvHom = [k]` with `k+k+1` annihilating every point, no remaining hypotheses). Resolves the `вЯ®GвЯ©`-vs-`E(рЭФљ_p)` scope ambiguity. Pure-kernel on top of the keystone
